title: dryopteris clintoniana f. atropalustris
intro: Dryopteris clintoniana f. atropalustris, also known as the black shield fern, is a subspecies of the Clinton's fern (Dryopteris clintoniana), a type of flowering plant in the wood fern family (Dryopteridaceae). The black shield fern has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other phenolic compounds. This plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the black shield fern is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be dried and used in capsules or powder form.
